params.name: Deep Navy Computer
params.description:
On Jan 21, 1997 AD, the Deep Navy Computer defeated a human chess grandmaster, winning two games, drawing three, and losing one.
It took the technicians six years to develop the Deep Navy Computer. This supercomputer is equipped with 31 parallel processors and can process data for 50 billion chess moves in three minutes. Although it cannot learn from experience or improvise, it still won two games against a human grandmaster.
